sections.chapter_title: Combination of PRA Senior Management Functions

rules.summary: This Chapter does not apply to a third country branch undertaking or to a firm that does not have an establishment in the UK.
A large firm must ensure that a person who performs the Chair of the Governing Body function on its behalf does not perform the Chief Executive function within the same firm.
A large firm that is a member of a group must ensure that a group executive does not perform any of the following functions in respect of the firm :
A group executive means, in relation to a large firm , a person who is an officer or employee of and performs an executive function at:

content_markdown:
1

###  Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a _UK Solvency II firm_ ;
  2. (2) in accordance with [Insurance General Application 3](/pra-rules/insurance-general-application#2e5a9cd32e6d4026895a5d543b990108 "Insurance General Application 3"), the _Society_ , as modified by [11](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#feddc4e9ca63445f828642a7fcd2e230 "11");
  3. (3) in accordance with [Insurance General Application 3](/pra-rules/insurance-general-application#2e5a9cd32e6d4026895a5d543b990108 "Insurance General Application 3"), _managing agents_ , as modified by [11](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#feddc4e9ca63445f828642a7fcd2e230 "11");
  4. (4) a _third country branch undertaking_ (other than a _Swiss general insurer_);
  5. (5) a _UK ISPV_ , in accordance with [12](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#169e4d6ef0ee490ab5c2ba15272547ad "12"); and
  6. (6) a _third country insurance services provider_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

Chair of the Governing Body function

has the meaning given in [4.1](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#79684898e212424ca41cdb3825f31ec8 "4.1").

Chair of Audit Committee function

has the meaning given in [4.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#511bb609ebd84468aba17c4d61a974d4 "4.3").

Chair of Remuneration Committee function

has the meaning given in [4.4](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#ec447ae6a2534cbab4da2e29860fd6c6 "4.4").

Chair of Risk Committee function

has the meaning given in [4.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#c301c64e6c1847a28863d6ecf44f4ec5 "4.2").

Chief Actuary function

has the meaning given in [7.1](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#cb1cba75ee114bee966e85c5d25040ca "7.1").

Chief Executive function

has the meaning given in [3.1](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#14f7c30c660c4d569eda50d13ed7486f "3.1") or [12.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#62188b2311a34bd499b94af12c426ce3) (as applicable).

Chief Finance function

has the meaning given in [3.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#8976f9e4cb6143149d1a89eed28a2a75 "3.2").

Chief Operations function

has the meaning given in [3.7](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#4e70a623331a49d68a8dea3c7baffc8f "3.7").

Chief Risk function

has the meaning given in [3.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#542ef69c8fe5422da6cf71d81c9d443b "3.3").

Chief Underwriting Officer function

has the meaning given in [9.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#ce96a42d105840ff8830dce1fa160a0d "9.2").

Group Entity Senior Manager function

has the meaning given in [5.1](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#45d813d3748b43ba9c606e3acdd5f00d "5.1").

group executive

has the meaning given in [13.4](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#8b85382f420e45edb0c2b34d28ccecd6 "13.4").

Head of Internal Audit function

has the meaning given in [3.4](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#adf5822148d64deea5f628c851905566 "3.4").

Head of Key Business Area function

has the meaning given in [3.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#be2e988cb148472a983dfabd1b5826c2 "3.5").

Head of small run-off firm function

has the meaning given in [6A.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#992f022295e34e3ab15cdc4df3a1dab1 "6A.2").

Head of Third Country Branch function

has the meaning given in [6.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#2daf49b9b2694c379e137381bf2fa023 "6.2").

Senior Independent Director function

has the meaning given in [4.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#b60c3a5b6d374e668e555bb843cdda5f "4.5").

Underwriting Risk Oversight function

has the meaning given in [10.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#0c9d9b79056c4d57a114881300fec63a "10.2").

With-Profits Actuary function

has the meaning given in [8.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#b943aca68a3748e1a2d30520fdbd20c5 "8.2").

*Effective: 24/07/2025*

2

###  General

2.1

Each of the functions in [3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#9762c33c597a45ab87f91098dfbec28f "3") – [10](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#f338baec50d143d9acb5707f58157964 "10") and [12](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#169e4d6ef0ee490ab5c2ba15272547ad) is a controlled function and a PRA senior management function.

*Effective: 24/07/2025*

2.2

_PRA senior management function holders_ must each be approved by the _PRA_ to perform the applicable _PRA senior management function_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.3

  1. (1) A firm (other than a third country branch undertaking, a firm that does not have an establishment in the UK, a small run-off firm, or a UK ISPV) must ensure that one or more persons performs each of the following PRA senior management functions on its behalf:
     1. (a) the Chief Executive function;
     2. (b) the Chief Finance function; and
     3. (c) the Chair of the Governing Body function.
  2. (2) If a vacancy arises in respect of one or more of the PRA senior management functions set out in (1), a firm must ensure that it appoints a person to fill that vacancy as soon as practicable.

*Effective: 24/07/2025*

2.4

To the extent that:

  1. (1) a _firm_ appoints a _person_ to perform a _key function_ which, but for this rule, would be a _PRA senior management function_ ;
  2. (2) the appointment is solely to provide cover for a _PRA senior management function holder_ whose absence is:
     1. (a) temporary; or
     2. (b) reasonably unforeseen; and
  3. (3) the appointment is for less than 12 weeks in a consecutive 12-month period,

the description of the applicable _PRA senior management function_ does not relate to those activities of that _person_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.5

  1. (1) If, in relation to a _firm_ , a _PRA senior management function holder_ also performs _FCA activities_ , performance of the _PRA senior management function_ will include the performance of those _FCA activities_ , provided the following conditions are met:
     1. (a) the _PRA’s_ approval to perform that _PRA senior management function_ has been granted and continues in force;
     2. (b) at the time of approval being granted by the _PRA_ , that _person_ was not subject to an _FCA approval_ to perform the particular _FCA governing function_ ;
     3. (c) the _firm_ made an _FCA activities notification_ ; and
     4. (d) that _person_ performs and is continuing to perform the _FCA activities_.
  2. (2) If, in relation to a _firm_ , a _PRA senior management function holder_ has been performing _FCA activities_ in the circumstances set out in (1), and that _person_ :
     1. (a) ceases to perform the _PRA senior management function_ ;
     2. (b) within three _months_ of ceasing performance, is approved to perform a new _PRA senior management function_ at the _firm_ ; and
     3. (c) continues to perform the _FCA activities_ ,
  3. performance of the new _PRA senior management function_ will include the performance of those _FCA activities_ , provided the conditions in (1) (a) to (d) are met.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.6

If a _PRA senior management function holder_ to whom [2.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#242e08595aa849bdb053fcce1554324e "2.5") applies ceases to perform a _PRA senior management function_ but continues to perform the _FCA activities_ , [2.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#242e08595aa849bdb053fcce1554324e "2.5")(1) will continue to apply in respect of the performance of the _FCA activities_ until the earlier of:

  1. (1) approval by the _FCA_ in respect of the performance by that _person_ of those _FCA activities_ as an _FCA governing function_ in relation to the _firm_ ; or
  2. (2) three _months_ from the time that the _person_ ceased to perform that _PRA senior management function_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.6A

If a _PRA approved person_ :

  1. (1) (other than in the circumstances set out in [2.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#242e08595aa849bdb053fcce1554324e "2.5") or [2.6](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#6541a50a03474941b8cfa652d81ecbd5 "2.6")) performs one or more _FCA responsibilities_ ;
  2. (2) ceases to perform any _PRA senior management function_ ; and
  3. (3) does not have an _FCA approval_ to perform an _FCA controlled function_ in relation to that _firm_ ,
  4. the functions in (1) will continue to be part of the _PRA senior management function_ that the _person_ most recently performed for that _firm_ until the earlier of:
     1. (a) approval by the _FCA_ in respect of the performance by that _person_ of an _FCA controlled function_ in relation to the _firm_ ; or
     2. (b) three _months_ from the time that the _person_ ceased to perform that _PRA senior management function_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.7

If a _firm_ that is not significant outsources its internal audit _function_ to an external third party service provider (that is, a service provider outside the _firm_ or the _firm’s_ _group_), the _firm_ is not required to have any _person(s)_ approved to perform the _Head of Internal Audit function_.

*Effective: 12/09/2017*

2.8

  1. (1) A _firm_ (other than a _UK ISPV_) must take all reasonable steps to ensure that before a _person_ begins to perform:
     1. (a) a _PRA senior management function_ ; or
     2. (b) new or revised responsibilities in performance of a _PRA senior management function_ ,
  2. that _person_ is provided with all of the information and materials that the _person_ may reasonably expect in order to perform that _PRA senior management function _or those new or revised responsibilities effectively and in accordance with the _regulatory system_.
  3. (2) A _firm_ must:
     1. (a) have a written policy to ensure compliance with (1), including in relation to the systems and controls it uses;
     2. (b) ensure the policy under (a) is complied with; and
     3. (c) maintain adequate records of the steps taken to comply with (1).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3

###  Executive

3.1

The _Chief Executive function_ (SMF1) is the function of having responsibility, under the immediate authority of the _governing body_ , alone or jointly with others, for carrying out the management of the conduct of the whole of the business (or relevant activities) of a _firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3.2

The _Chief Finance function_ (SMF2) is the function of having responsibility for the management of the financial resources of a _firm_ and reporting to the _governing body_ of a _firm_ in relation to its financial affairs.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3.3

The _Chief Risk function_ (SMF4) is the function of having responsibility for overall management of the risk management system specified in [Conditions Governing Business 3](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#73c8c58e1d8843858d048ba7be5be7fb "Conditions Governing Business 3").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3.4

The _Head of Internal Audit function_ (SMF5) is the function of having responsibility for the management of the internal audit _function_ specified in [Conditions Governing Business 5](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#839b41b0b1654232bd881e8955b1bb27 "Conditions Governing Business 5").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3.5

The _Head of Key Business Area function_ (SMF6) is the function of having responsibility for management of a business area or division of a _firm_ (other than a _third country branch undertaking_) where:

  1. (1) the relevant business area or division of the _firm_ accounts for more than 20% of the _firm’s_ gross revenue; and
  2. (2) the _person_ performing that function does not report to a _person_ performing the _Head of Key Business Area function_ in respect of that same business area or division of the _firm_ ; and
  3. (3) either the gross total assets or the _technical provisions_ of the relevant business area or division of the _firm_ exceed £10 billion.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3.6

For the purposes of [3.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#be2e988cb148472a983dfabd1b5826c2 "3.5"), the gross total assets and _technical provisions_ of the business area or division of the _firm_ and the percentage of the gross revenue of the _firm_ shall be assessed on the basis of either:

  1. (1) the assets, _technical provisions_ and revenues for the business area or division and the _firm_ for the _firm’s_ financial year immediately preceding that in which the _person_ is allocated with the specified responsibilities; or
  2. (2) if the threshold amount is not met for that period, on the basis of the annual average amount assessed across a rolling period of five years, assessed by reference to the _firm’s_ annual accounting date; and where the _firm_ or the business area or division has been in existence for less than five years, the assessment will be made on the basis of the annual average amount for the period during which the _firm_ or that business area or division has existed (calculated by reference to the _firm’s_ annual accounting date).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3.7

The _Chief Operations function_ (SMF24) is the function of having responsibility for the internal operations and technology of a _firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4

###  Oversight

4.1

The _Chair of the Governing Body function_ (SMF9) is the function of having responsibility for chairing, and overseeing the performance of the _governing body_ of a _firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4.2

The _Chair of Risk Committee function_ (SMF10) is the function of having responsibility for chairing, and overseeing the performance of any committee responsible for the oversight of the risk management system specified in [Conditions Governing Business 3](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#73c8c58e1d8843858d048ba7be5be7fb "Conditions Governing Business 3").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4.3

The _Chair of Audit Committee function_ (SMF11) is the function of having responsibility for chairing and overseeing the performance of any committee responsible for the oversight of the internal audit _function_ specified in [Conditions Governing Business 5](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#839b41b0b1654232bd881e8955b1bb27 "Conditions Governing Business 5").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4.4

The _Chair of Remuneration Committee function_ (SMF12) is the function of having responsibility for chairing and overseeing the performance of any committee responsible for the oversight of the design or implementation of the remuneration policies and practices of a _firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4.5

The _Senior Independent Director function_ (SMF14) is the function of performing the role of a senior independent _director_ , and having particular responsibility for leading the assessment of performance of the _person_ performing the _Chair of the Governing Body function_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5

###  Group Entities

5.1

The _Group Entity Senior Manager function_ (SMF7) is the function of having a significant influence on the management or conduct of one or more aspects of the affairs of a _firm_ in relation to its _regulated activities_ (other than in the course of the performance of another _PRA senior management function_) and which is performed by a _person_ employed by, or an officer of:

  1. (1) a _parent undertaking_ or holding company of a _firm_ ; or
  2. (2) another _undertaking_ which is a member of the _firm’s_ _group_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

6

###  Head of Third Country Branch

6.1

This Chapter applies only to a _firm_ that is a _third country branch undertaking_ or a _third country insurance services provider_ (other than a _Swiss general insurer_).

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.1A

  1. (1) [6.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#2daf49b9b2694c379e137381bf2fa023 "6.2"), [6.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#f05ea270aa044a73937b6538886e72d9 "6.3"), [6.4](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#bd249ae61e684727b37522ec62adf2ff "6.4") and [6.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#9b21e31c773d48e48c6b9b4e2588bf86 "6.5") shall not apply to a _SRO firm_.
  2. (2) [6.6](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#56c9c7f3f6554f3bb882b0da341a2ce6 "6.6") and [6.7](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#a96c11534b444e5b9d6097bf4901dd36 "6.7") apply only to a _SRO firm_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.2

The _Head of Third Country Branch function_ (SMF19) is the function of having responsibility for:

  1. (1) the conduct of all activities of the _third country branch undertaking_ that are subject to the _regulatory system_ ; or
  2. (2) the conduct of all activities of the _third country insurance services provider_ that are subject to the _regulatory system_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.3

  1. (1) A _third country branch undertaking_ or a _third country insurance services provider_ must have at least one _person_ approved to perform the _Head of Third Country Branch function_.
  2. (2) If a vacancy arises in respect of the _Head of Third Country Branch function_ , a _third country branch undertaking _or a _third country insurance services provider_ must ensure that it appoints a _person_ to fill that vacancy as soon as possible.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.4

A _third country branch undertaking_ or a _third country insurance services provider_ that transacts _with-profits insurance business_ must have at least one _person_ approved to perform the _With-Profits Actuary function_ (SMF20a).

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.5

A _third country branch undertaking _or a _third country insurance services provider_ is not required to have any _person_(s) approved to perform any of the other _PRA senior management functions_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.6

A _SRO firm_ must ensure that at least one _person_ performs the _Head of Third Country Branch Function_ on its behalf and if a vacancy arises in respect of that function it must ensure that it appoints a _person_ to fill the vacancy as soon as possible.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.7

For the purposes of [6.6](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-management-functions#56c9c7f3f6554f3bb882b0da341a2ce6 "6.6") the _Head of Third Country Branch Function_ (SMF 19) is the function of having responsibility to oversee the orderly run-off of the _firm_ 's _regulated activities_ in the _UK_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6A

###  Head of Small Run-off Firm

6A.1

This Chapter applies only to a _small run-off firm_.

*Effective: 12/09/2017*

6A.2

The _Head of small run-off firm function_ (SMF26) is the function of:

  1. (1) having responsibility for the conduct of the _regulated activities_ ; or
  2. (2) chairing the _governing body_

of a _small run-off firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

6A.3

  1. (1) A _small run-off firm_ must ensure that one or more _persons_ performs the _Head of small run-off firm function_ or the _Chief Executive function_ on its behalf.
  2. (2) A _small run-off firm_ must ensure that there is a _person_ appointed to the _Head of small run-off firm function_ or the _Chief Executive function_ who has ultimate responsibility for the conduct of the _regulated activities_ of the _small run-off firm_.
  3. (3) If a vacancy arises in respect of that aspect of the _Head of small run-off firm function_ or the _Chief Executive function_ referred to in (2) above, a _small run-off firm_ must ensure that it appoints a _person_ to fill that vacancy as soon as practicable.

*Effective: 12/09/2017*

6A.4

A _small run-off firm_ must have at least one _person_ approved to perform the _Chief Finance function_ (SMF2).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

6A.5

A _small run-off firm_ that transacts _with-profits insurance business_ must have at least one _person_ approved to perform the _With-Profits Actuary function_ (SMF20a).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

6A.6

A _small run-off firm_ is not required to have any _person_(s) approved to perform any of the other _PRA senior management functions_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

7

###  Chief Actuary

7.1

The _Chief Actuary function_ (SMF20) is the function of having responsibility for the actuarial _function_ specified in [Conditions Governing Business 6](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#f0974bf00748415e922ba203d6a6a0f7 "Conditions Governing Business 6").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

8

###  With-Profits Actuary

8.1

This Chapter applies only to _firms_ that carry on _with-profits insurance business_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

8.2

The _With-Profits Actuary function_ (SMF20a) is the function of having responsibility for advising the _governing body_ of a _firm_ transacting _with-profits insurance business_ on the exercise of discretion affecting part or all of that business, as described more fully in [Actuaries 5.1](/pra-rules/actuaries#3816ec8c65a447b7b2f6f260f0c3166a "Actuaries 5.1").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

9

###  Chief Underwriting Officer

9.1

This Chapter applies only to _firms_ that carry on _general insurance business_ and to _managing agents_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

9.2

The _Chief Underwriting Officer function_ (SMF23) is the function of having responsibility, in respect of the _firm’s_ _general insurance business_ , for the underwriting decisions in respect of material insurance risks that:

  1. (1) in relation to _firms_ that carry on _general insurance business_ , are borne by the _firm_ ; or
  2. (2) in relation to _managing agents_ , are borne by _members_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

10

###  Underwriting Risk Oversight

10.1

This Chapter applies only to the _Society_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

10.2

The _Underwriting Risk Oversight function_ (SMF23a) is the function of overseeing and influencing underwriting plans by _managing agents_ in respect of risks borne by _members_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

11

###  Lloyd’s

11.1

This Part applies to the _Society_ and _managing agents_ separately.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

12

###  UK ISPVs

12.1

This Chapter applies only to _firms_ that are _UK ISPVs_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

12.2

[Deleted]

*Effective: 24/07/2025*

12.3

The Chief Executive function (SMF1) for a UK ISPV is the function of having responsibility for:

  1. (1) the conduct of all activities of the UK ISPV that are subject to the regulatory system; and
  2. (2) where the UK ISPV has a services provider, the conduct of all activities of the services provider that are subject to the regulatory system.

*Effective: 24/07/2025*

12.4

  1. (1) A UK ISPV must have at least one person approved to perform the Chief Executive function.
  2. (2) If a vacancy arises in respect of the Chief Executive function, the UK ISPV must ensure that it appoints a person to fill that vacancy as soon as possible.

*Effective: 24/07/2025*

12.5

A UK ISPV is not required to have any person(s) approved to perform any of the other PRA senior management functions.

*Effective: 24/07/2025*

13

###  Combination of PRA Senior Management Functions

13.1

This Chapter does not apply to a _third country branch undertaking _or to a _firm_ that does not have an establishment in the _UK_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

13.2

A _large firm_ must ensure that a _person_ who performs the _Chair of the Governing Body function_ on its behalf does not perform the _Chief Executive function_ within the same _firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

13.3

A _large firm_ that is a member of a _group_ must ensure that a _group executive_ does not perform any of the following functions in respect of the _firm_ :

  1. (1) the _Chair of the Governing Body function_ (SMF9);
  2. (2) the _Chair of Risk Committee function_ (SMF10);
  3. (3) the _Chair of Audit Committee function_ (SMF11);
  4. (4) the _Chair of Remuneration Committee function_ (SMF12); or
  5. (5) the _Senior Independent Director function_ (SMF14).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

13.4

A _group executive_ means, in relation to a _large firm_ , a _person_ who is an _officer_ or _employee_ of and performs an executive function at:

  1. (1) a _parent undertaking_ or holding company of the _large firm_ ; or
  2. (2) an undertaking which is a member of the same _group_ as the _large firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*
content_html:
1

Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a UK Solvency II firm;
  2. (2) in accordance with Insurance General Application 3, the Society, as modified by 11;
  3. (3) in accordance with Insurance General Application 3, managing agents, as modified by 11;
  4. (4) a third country branch undertaking (other than a Swiss general insurer);
  5. (5) a UK ISPV, in accordance with 12; and
  6. (6) a third country insurance services provider.
  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 1.1 before 31/12/2020

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

Chair of the Governing Body function

has the meaning given in 4.1.

Chair of Audit Committee function

has the meaning given in 4.3.

Chair of Remuneration Committee function

has the meaning given in 4.4.

Chair of Risk Committee function

has the meaning given in 4.2.

Chief Actuary function

has the meaning given in 7.1.

Chief Executive function

has the meaning given in 3.1 or 12.3 (as applicable).

Chief Finance function

has the meaning given in 3.2.

Chief Operations function

has the meaning given in 3.7.

Chief Risk function

has the meaning given in 3.3.

Chief Underwriting Officer function

has the meaning given in 9.2.

Group Entity Senior Manager function

has the meaning given in 5.1.

group executive

has the meaning given in 13.4.

Head of Internal Audit function

has the meaning given in 3.4.

Head of Key Business Area function

has the meaning given in 3.5.

Head of small run-off firm function

has the meaning given in 6A.2.

Head of Third Country Branch function

has the meaning given in 6.2.

Senior Independent Director function

has the meaning given in 4.5.

Underwriting Risk Oversight function

has the meaning given in 10.2.

With-Profits Actuary function

has the meaning given in 8.2.

  • 24/07/2025
  • Past version of 1.2 before 24/07/2025

2

General

2.1

Each of the functions in 3 – 10 and 12 is a controlled function and a PRA senior management function.

  • 24/07/2025
  • Past version of 2.1 before 24/07/2025

2.2

PRA senior management function holders must each be approved by the PRA to perform the applicable PRA senior management function.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.2 before 10/12/2018

2.3

  1. (1) A firm (other than a third country branch undertaking, a firm that does not have an establishment in the UK, a small run-off firm, or a UK ISPV) must ensure that one or more persons performs each of the following PRA senior management functions on its behalf:
    1. (a) the Chief Executive function;
    2. (b) the Chief Finance function; and
    3. (c) the Chair of the Governing Body function.
  2. (2) If a vacancy arises in respect of one or more of the PRA senior management functions set out in (1), a firm must ensure that it appoints a person to fill that vacancy as soon as practicable.
  • 24/07/2025
  • Past version of 2.3 before 24/07/2025

2.4

To the extent that:

  1. (1) a firm appoints a person to perform a key function which, but for this rule, would be a PRA senior management function;
  2. (2) the appointment is solely to provide cover for a PRA senior management function holder whose absence is:
    1. (a) temporary; or
    2. (b) reasonably unforeseen; and
  3. (3) the appointment is for less than 12 weeks in a consecutive 12-month period,

the description of the applicable PRA senior management function does not relate to those activities of that person.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.4 before 10/12/2018

2.5

  1. (1) If, in relation to a firm, a PRA senior management function holder also performs FCA activities, performance of the PRA senior management function will include the performance of those FCA activities, provided the following conditions are met:
    1. (a) the PRA’s approval to perform that PRA senior management function has been granted and continues in force;
    2. (b) at the time of approval being granted by the PRA, that person was not subject to an FCA approval to perform the particular FCA governing function;
    3. (c) the firm made an FCA activities notification; and
    4. (d) that person performs and is continuing to perform the FCA activities.
  2. (2) If, in relation to a firm, a PRA senior management function holder has been performing FCA activities in the circumstances set out in (1), and that person:
    1. (a) ceases to perform the PRA senior management function;
    2. (b) within three months of ceasing performance, is approved to perform a new PRA senior management function at the firm; and
    3. (c) continues to perform the FCA activities,
  3. performance of the new PRA senior management function will include the performance of those FCA activities, provided the conditions in (1) (a) to (d) are met.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.5 before 10/12/2018

2.6

If a PRA senior management function holder to whom 2.5 applies ceases to perform a PRA senior management function but continues to perform the FCA activities, 2.5(1) will continue to apply in respect of the performance of the FCA activities until the earlier of:

  1. (1) approval by the FCA in respect of the performance by that person of those FCA activities as an FCA governing function in relation to the firm; or
  2. (2) three months from the time that the person ceased to perform that PRA senior management function.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.6 before 10/12/2018

2.6A

If a PRA approved person:

  1. (1) (other than in the circumstances set out in 2.5 or 2.6) performs one or more FCA responsibilities;
  2. (2) ceases to perform any PRA senior management function; and
  3. (3) does not have an FCA approval to perform an FCA controlled function in relation to that firm,
  4. the functions in (1) will continue to be part of the PRA senior management function that the person most recently performed for that firm until the earlier of:
    1. (a) approval by the FCA in respect of the performance by that person of an FCA controlled function in relation to the firm; or
    2. (b) three months from the time that the person ceased to perform that PRA senior management function.
  • 10/12/2018

2.7

If a firm that is not significant outsources its internal audit function to an external third party service provider (that is, a service provider outside the firm or the firm’s group), the firm is not required to have any person(s) approved to perform the Head of Internal Audit function.

  • 12/09/2017

2.8

  1. (1) A firm (other than a UK ISPV) must take all reasonable steps to ensure that before a person begins to perform:
    1. (a) a PRA senior management function; or
    2. (b) new or revised responsibilities in performance of a PRA senior management function,
  2. that person is provided with all of the information and materials that the person may reasonably expect in order to perform that PRA senior management function or those new or revised responsibilities effectively and in accordance with the regulatory system.
  3. (2) A firm must:
    1. (a) have a written policy to ensure compliance with (1), including in relation to the systems and controls it uses;
    2. (b) ensure the policy under (a) is complied with; and
    3. (c) maintain adequate records of the steps taken to comply with (1).
  • 10/12/2018

3

Executive

3.1

The Chief Executive function (SMF1) is the function of having responsibility, under the immediate authority of the governing body, alone or jointly with others, for carrying out the management of the conduct of the whole of the business (or relevant activities) of a firm.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.1 before 10/12/2018

3.2

The Chief Finance function (SMF2) is the function of having responsibility for the management of the financial resources of a firm and reporting to the governing body of a firm in relation to its financial affairs.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.2 before 10/12/2018

3.3

The Chief Risk function (SMF4) is the function of having responsibility for overall management of the risk management system specified in Conditions Governing Business 3.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.3 before 10/12/2018

3.4

The Head of Internal Audit function (SMF5) is the function of having responsibility for the management of the internal audit function specified in Conditions Governing Business 5.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.4 before 10/12/2018

3.5

The Head of Key Business Area function (SMF6) is the function of having responsibility for management of a business area or division of a firm (other than a third country branch undertaking) where:

  1. (1) the relevant business area or division of the firm accounts for more than 20% of the firm’s gross revenue; and
  2. (2) the person performing that function does not report to a person performing the Head of Key Business Area function in respect of that same business area or division of the firm; and
  3. (3) either the gross total assets or the technical provisions of the relevant business area or division of the firm exceed £10 billion.
  • 10/12/2018

3.6

For the purposes of 3.5, the gross total assets and technical provisions of the business area or division of the firm and the percentage of the gross revenue of the firm shall be assessed on the basis of either:

  1. (1) the assets, technical provisions and revenues for the business area or division and the firm for the firm’s financial year immediately preceding that in which the person is allocated with the specified responsibilities; or
  2. (2) if the threshold amount is not met for that period, on the basis of the annual average amount assessed across a rolling period of five years, assessed by reference to the firm’s annual accounting date; and where the firm or the business area or division has been in existence for less than five years, the assessment will be made on the basis of the annual average amount for the period during which the firm or that business area or division has existed (calculated by reference to the firm’s annual accounting date).
  • 10/12/2018

3.7

The Chief Operations function (SMF24) is the function of having responsibility for the internal operations and technology of a firm.

  • 10/12/2018

4

Oversight

4.1

The Chair of the Governing Body function (SMF9) is the function of having responsibility for chairing, and overseeing the performance of the governing body of a firm.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 4.1 before 10/12/2018

4.2

The Chair of Risk Committee function (SMF10) is the function of having responsibility for chairing, and overseeing the performance of any committee responsible for the oversight of the risk management system specified in Conditions Governing Business 3.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 4.2 before 10/12/2018

4.3

The Chair of Audit Committee function (SMF11) is the function of having responsibility for chairing and overseeing the performance of any committee responsible for the oversight of the internal audit function specified in Conditions Governing Business 5.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 4.3 before 10/12/2018

4.4

The Chair of Remuneration Committee function (SMF12) is the function of having responsibility for chairing and overseeing the performance of any committee responsible for the oversight of the design or implementation of the remuneration policies and practices of a firm.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 4.4 before 10/12/2018

4.5

The Senior Independent Director function (SMF14) is the function of performing the role of a senior independent director, and having particular responsibility for leading the assessment of performance of the person performing the Chair of the Governing Body function.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 4.5 before 10/12/2018

5

Group Entities

5.1

The Group Entity Senior Manager function (SMF7) is the function of having a significant influence on the management or conduct of one or more aspects of the affairs of a firm in relation to its regulated activities (other than in the course of the performance of another PRA senior management function) and which is performed by a person employed by, or an officer of:

  1. (1) a parent undertaking or holding company of a firm; or
  2. (2) another undertaking which is a member of the firm’s group.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.1 before 10/12/2018

6

Head of Third Country Branch

6.1

This Chapter applies only to a firm that is a third country branch undertaking or a third country insurance services provider (other than a Swiss general insurer).

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 6.1 before 31/12/2020

6.1A

  1. (1) 6.2, 6.3, 6.4 and 6.5 shall not apply to a SRO firm.
  2. (2) 6.6 and 6.7 apply only to a SRO firm.
  • 31/12/2020

6.2

The Head of Third Country Branch function (SMF19) is the function of having responsibility for:

  1. (1) the conduct of all activities of the third country branch undertaking that are subject to the regulatory system; or
  2. (2) the conduct of all activities of the third country insurance services provider that are subject to the regulatory system.
  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 6.2 before 31/12/2020

6.3

  1. (1) A third country branch undertaking or a third country insurance services provider must have at least one person approved to perform the Head of Third Country Branch function.
  2. (2) If a vacancy arises in respect of the Head of Third Country Branch function, a third country branch undertaking or a third country insurance services provider must ensure that it appoints a person to fill that vacancy as soon as possible.
  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 6.3 before 31/12/2020

6.4

A third country branch undertaking or a third country insurance services provider that transacts with-profits insurance business must have at least one person approved to perform the With-Profits Actuary function (SMF20a).

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 6.4 before 31/12/2020

6.5

A third country branch undertaking or a third country insurance services provider is not required to have any person(s) approved to perform any of the other PRA senior management functions.

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 6.5 before 31/12/2020

6.6

A SRO firm must ensure that at least one person performs the Head of Third Country Branch Function on its behalf and if a vacancy arises in respect of that function it must ensure that it appoints a person to fill the vacancy as soon as possible.

  • 31/12/2020

6.7

For the purposes of 6.6 the Head of Third Country Branch Function (SMF 19) is the function of having responsibility to oversee the orderly run-off of the firm's regulated activities in the UK.

  • 31/12/2020

6A

Head of Small Run-off Firm

6A.1

This Chapter applies only to a small run-off firm.

  • 12/09/2017

6A.2

The Head of small run-off firm function (SMF26) is the function of:

  1. (1) having responsibility for the conduct of the regulated activities; or
  2. (2) chairing the governing body

of a small run-off firm.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 6A.2 before 10/12/2018

6A.3

  1. (1) A small run-off firm must ensure that one or more persons performs the Head of small run-off firm function or the Chief Executive function on its behalf.
  2. (2) A small run-off firm must ensure that there is a person appointed to the Head of small run-off firm function or the Chief Executive function who has ultimate responsibility for the conduct of the regulated activities of the small run-off firm.
  3. (3) If a vacancy arises in respect of that aspect of the Head of small run-off firm function or the Chief Executive function referred to in (2) above, a small run-off firm must ensure that it appoints a person to fill that vacancy as soon as practicable.
  • 12/09/2017

6A.4

A small run-off firm must have at least one person approved to perform the Chief Finance function (SMF2).

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 6A.4 before 10/12/2018

6A.5

A small run-off firm that transacts with-profits insurance business must have at least one person approved to perform the With-Profits Actuary function (SMF20a).

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 6A.5 before 10/12/2018

6A.6

A small run-off firm is not required to have any person(s) approved to perform any of the other PRA senior management functions.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 6A.6 before 10/12/2018

7

Chief Actuary

7.1

The Chief Actuary function (SMF20) is the function of having responsibility for the actuarial function specified in Conditions Governing Business 6.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 7.1 before 10/12/2018

8

With-Profits Actuary

8.1

This Chapter applies only to firms that carry on with-profits insurance business.

  • 07/03/2016

8.2

The With-Profits Actuary function (SMF20a) is the function of having responsibility for advising the governing body of a firm transacting with-profits insurance business on the exercise of discretion affecting part or all of that business, as described more fully in Actuaries 5.1.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 8.2 before 10/12/2018

9

Chief Underwriting Officer

9.1

This Chapter applies only to firms that carry on general insurance business and to managing agents.

  • 07/03/2016

9.2

The Chief Underwriting Officer function (SMF23) is the function of having responsibility, in respect of the firm’s general insurance business, for the underwriting decisions in respect of material insurance risks that:

  1. (1) in relation to firms that carry on general insurance business, are borne by the firm; or
  2. (2) in relation to managing agents, are borne by members.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 9.2 before 10/12/2018

10

Underwriting Risk Oversight

10.1

This Chapter applies only to the Society.

  • 07/03/2016

10.2

The Underwriting Risk Oversight function (SMF23a) is the function of overseeing and influencing underwriting plans by managing agents in respect of risks borne by members.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 10.2 before 10/12/2018

11

Lloyd’s

11.1

This Part applies to the Society and managing agents separately.

  • 07/03/2016

12

UK ISPVs

12.1

This Chapter applies only to firms that are UK ISPVs.

  • 07/03/2016

12.2

[Deleted]

  • 24/07/2025
  • Past version of 12.2 before 24/07/2025

12.3

The Chief Executive function (SMF1) for a UK ISPV is the function of having responsibility for:

  1. (1) the conduct of all activities of the UK ISPV that are subject to the regulatory system; and
  2. (2) where the UK ISPV has a services provider, the conduct of all activities of the services provider that are subject to the regulatory system.
  • 24/07/2025

12.4

  1. (1) A UK ISPV must have at least one person approved to perform the Chief Executive function.
  2. (2) If a vacancy arises in respect of the Chief Executive function, the UK ISPV must ensure that it appoints a person to fill that vacancy as soon as possible.
  • 24/07/2025

12.5

A UK ISPV is not required to have any person(s) approved to perform any of the other PRA senior management functions.

  • 24/07/2025

13

Combination of PRA Senior Management Functions

13.1

This Chapter does not apply to a third country branch undertaking or to a firm that does not have an establishment in the UK.

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 13.1 before 31/12/2020

13.2

A large firm must ensure that a person who performs the Chair of the Governing Body function on its behalf does not perform the Chief Executive function within the same firm.

  • 10/12/2018

13.3

A large firm that is a member of a group must ensure that a group executive does not perform any of the following functions in respect of the firm:

  1. (1) the Chair of the Governing Body function (SMF9);
  2. (2) the Chair of Risk Committee function (SMF10);
  3. (3) the Chair of Audit Committee function (SMF11);
  4. (4) the Chair of Remuneration Committee function (SMF12); or
  5. (5) the Senior Independent Director function (SMF14).
  • 10/12/2018

13.4

A group executive means, in relation to a large firm, a person who is an officer or employee of and performs an executive function at:

  1. (1) a parent undertaking or holding company of the large firm; or
  2. (2) an undertaking which is a member of the same group as the large firm.
  • 10/12/2018
